key_insights.insight: Consistency and persistence are often more important than pure talent or skill.
key_insights.quote: But outlasting people and consistency is massively underrated.

key_insights.insight: Fear, when understood, can be a powerful motivator.
key_insights.quote: But now that I understand fear and what it does for me, it just fuels me so much in a way that nothing else can.

key_insights.insight: Mental preparation is often as, or even more, important than physical preparation, especially during high-pressure moments.
key_insights.quote: On Fight Night, ...I believe it completely flips and becomes about 80-plus percent mental.

key_insights.insight: Visualizing success and writing down goals, even sporadically, can influence your subconscious and make those goals feel more attainable.
key_insights.quote: It's as simple as getting a piece of paper, writing down this week I will do this and enjoy it... This year I will win two fights. I will enjoy both of them and I will perform to my best.

key_insights.insight: Hypnotherapy can be a valuable tool for managing anxiety and promoting a more tranquil state of mind, which can be particularly beneficial in high-stress environments.
key_insights.quote: But for me personally, it brings my anxiety down a lot. So it helps with that. It also helps with sleep. It also helps with just being just in a more tranquil place in general.

key_insights.insight: It's crucial for individuals to be aware of how to defend themselves, and it's a worthwhile skill regardless of career path.
key_insights.quote: But you need to have a general idea of how to defend yourself if it ever happens.

key_insights.insight: Surrounding yourself with the right people and eliminating negativity is essential for achieving goals and maintaining mental well-being.
key_insights.quote: Is that I completely cut out anything negativethat was in my life. Anything that wasn't, anything or anyonethat wasn't serving me or anyone that wasn'tserving me to become like run parallel with my journey to becoming the best heavyweight in the world, I completely cut off.

key_insights.insight: Sometimes, success doesn't immediately solve your problems or change who you are, but it can offer a different set of challenges.
key_insights.quote: Like, money, fame, and titles doesn't change muchin terms of what goes on inside your brain, in my opinion.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice routine-based visualization, mentally drilling situations to feel more prepared.
actionable_advice.how_to: Mentally rehearse scenarios, like walking out to a fight, repeatedly to normalize the experience.

actionable_advice.advice: Use paper-based affirmations to set goals and achieve them.
actionable_advice.how_to: Write down your weekly and yearly goals and read them periodically.

actionable_advice.advice: Implement recovery tactics to increase overall work ethic and athletic gains.
actionable_advice.how_to: Dedicate half of your training time to activities that reduce stress and promote muscle regeneration.

actionable_advice.advice: Start martial arts classes as a means to boost discipline, self-respect, and focus.
actionable_advice.how_to: Enroll in classes and focus on the individual gains to promote positive change.

actionable_advice.advice: Fake confidence to become more confident
actionable_advice.how_to: Walk around with a smile on your face and with your head held high and your shoulders back to change your mood

memorable_stories: Borrowing money to buy nappies while chasing his fighting dream.
The pivotal moment of his career as his knee gave out during a title eliminator fight.
contrarian_claims: Fear is an asset, not a liability, for high-performing athletes.
MMA fighters, especially at the higher weight classes, can get away with less intense nutritional monitoring than conventional wisdom dictates
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Tom Aspinall: The UFC Doesn't Want You To Know This! Jon Jones Wasn't Living Like An Athlete!
